title: Chapter 32
text: Chapter Twenty - Nine S he floated in darkness. It wracked her with agony, swallowed her, and she longed to cry out in pain. But she had no mouth, no lungs. There was nothing but her pain and the endless void . Until a voice penetrated the black . Don’t leave yet . Warmth bloomed through her, chasing away the icy cold for a fleeting moment. She knew that voice; it comforted her, shining like a beacon, giving her something to hold on to . When it spoke, shimmering blue flickered through the darkness, like the rolling waves of an ocean she’d never seen. But the light was gone so quickly it might never have existed at all . The pain came roaring back when the voice was silent, and she suffered alone. There were other voices, distant, muffled, and unfamiliar, and she shrunk from them. They came often, sometimes alone, sometimes in groups . Time had no meaning. She didn’t have a name, didn’t have a memory. But she had that voice to cling to. That voice to anchor her, the keep her from spiraling away into oblivion. It was the only thing to ease her pain, the only thing to look forward to . Don’t leave yet . She didn’t want to leave him yet. Not yet. Not even when the pain reached its worst, and the other voices grew louder, and the darkness thickened impossibly . Don’t leave yet . The words echoed through the void and she grasped at them. She didn’t want to be taken away, she refused . Don’t leave yet . Lara opened her eyes, and it was the hardest thing she’d ever done. Vision blurred, she battled her heavy eyelids and stared into blinding white light. Something beeped to her right, slow and rhythmic. She blinked; the room came into focus slowly, and the shapes didn’t make sense. The unfamiliar voices were clear , now . “How long is this going to take?” A man asked from nearby . “I can’t say for sure,” a woman replied. “She’ll wake up when her mind and body are ready .” “It’s been three days, and she hasn’t so much as wiggled a finger .” Focusing all her will through her confusion and pain, Lara twitched a finger . “We can’t just force her to wake up, Dave. That’s not how it works .” “She might never wake up at all, Nancy .” “That’s a possibility. But I’m not — ” “Every moment we keep her here is a drain on our resources. Those machines draw power from a limited pool, and nobody’s making new medical supplies anymore. She’s an outsider, and she’s taking up the time of our medical staff .” “She’s used hardly anything so far, and she’s a human being . Isn’t a life still worth more than a few plastic tubes and bit of my time?” Nancy demanded quietly . “And if she doesn’t come out of it? The cost only adds up over days and weeks. She’s going to need to be fed, and that’ll cause more complications. We can’t allow this to go on indefinitely .” “That’s not your call to make, Dave. And you’re stupid if you think her companion will let you cut her off .” “He couldn’t lay a hand on me,” Dave said uncertainly . “You think rules will stop him ?” Who were they? Where was she ? Lara blinked again, and dragged her gaze around the room. The blinding white dissolved into differing shades — the ceiling, the cloth, the walls, the sheet draped over her legs, all subtly varied whites. Nothing real could be so pure, so clean . The nearby beeping continued, picking up speed and volume. She parted her dry lips and winced as they split. Sliding out her tongue, she tasted blood. When she lifted her right arm, something tugged at it, producing a sharp pain in the back of her hand. Her left arm was wrapped up tight and slung to her neck. When she tried to move it, a bone-deep ache throbbed up to her shoulder . She turned her head, following the tubes and wires that ran from her arm up to the machines near the bed . What the fuck ? “She’s awake!” Nancy exclaimed. “Go get Ronin !” People crowded around the bed, their features obscured by shadow as their bodies blocked the light. They all seemed to speak at once, rapidly, using words Lara didn’t understand . The beeping continued to gain speed, and her chest hurt, each breath more labored than the last. She lifted her right hand, moving it closer to her left. She needed to pull the foreign things from her body. Needed to get away from these people, needed air … “Hold her down, before she hurts herself !” Hands fell upon Lara and pressed her onto the bed . “Lara,” Nancy said, “my name is — oh, no, don’t !” Lara yanked her arm. There was a moment of resistance, a flare of pain, and then the wires pulled free. The beeping became a monotone, ceaseless drone . She couldn’t breathe. Her lungs were on fire . The hands closed around her arms and legs, dragging her down. Lara fought them. Agony swept through her body . Warlord stood above her, fist swinging toward her face, raining blow after blow . Lara screamed. She thrashed, battling the strangers’ holds. They’d deactivated Ronin, took him from her, and Warlord kept hitting her, over and over, and the pain wouldn’t end. Her throat was raw from her screams, but the hands wouldn’t relent . “Lara, I’m Nancy, I’ve been — ” Lara’s right arm slipped from their hold and she swung her fist. Nancy cried out, stumbling back, blood oozing from her lip. Hands clamped down on Lara’s forearm and forced it down onto the bed . “Are you okay?” someone asked . “I’m fine,” Nancy replied. “We need her calmed down before she does herself more damage .” “What’s happening ? Lara ?” Lara stilled at the sound of that voice. Eyes wide, she turned her head to look between the strangers, finally catching sight of Ronin as he approached. He was here. She wanted to leap at him, to throw her arms around him, but even if she weren’t restrained, she wasn’t sure she had the strength . “Ronin,” she sobbed . The strangers shifted aside as he fell onto his knees beside the bed. He gently cradled her cheeks in his hands, spreading warmth through her face . “I’m here,” he said. “ You’re safe .” The restraining hands lifted away. Pulses of sharp pain flowed through Lara’s exhausted body, but she ignored them. He was here with her. That was all that mattered . “I need to see her arm,” Nancy said . “No one here means you harm, Lara,” Ronin said, shifting aside to allow Nancy access without breaking contact . Nancy’s cool fingers closed lightly around Lara’s wrist, turning it. She looked over her shoulder, speaking to the others . Lara stared up into Ronin’s eyes. It was only because of his steadiness that she remained still as Nancy softly prodded her hand . “Where are we? Where’s Warlord?” she rasped . “We’re safe. Trust me on that.” He smoothed back her hair. He didn’t look away as Nancy wrapped Lara’s bleeding hand in a bandage . “We’ll be fine,” Nancy said. The other strangers exited through the curtain and pulled it closed . “Look at me,” Ronin said. Lara forced her gaze back up to him. “These people are helping you .” “Where are we, Ronin ?” His fingers trailed along her hairline, down her cheeks, calming her . “Lara,” Nancy said, “I’d like you to drink this. It will help with the pain .” Lara looked at the dark-haired woman and the tiny cup she offered. When she glanced back at Ronin, he nodded. Whoever Nancy was, Ronin trusted her, and that’d have to be enough for now. She took the cup and stared down at the reddish-brown liquid inside. It had a strange odor, similar to the booze at Kitty’s, but something was off about it. Her instincts said don’t drink it . “Best to swallow it quickly,” Nancy said, offering a reassuring smile . Raising the cup to her lips, Lara hesitated for a second before pouring it into her mouth and swallowing quickly . It burned like fire going down her throat. The flavor of the alcohol fled quickly, overpowered by a bitter aftertaste worse than anything she’d ever had. She coughed, wincing, and pain exploded through her chest . “What the hell was that?” she demanded through clenched teeth, taking shallow breaths. Anything deeper produced a sharp pain in her side . “Laudanum, or as close to it as we can manage. Our options for pain management are limited, but most people say the relief is worth the taste, once it kicks in.” Nancy settled a soft hand on Lara’s shoulder. “Get some rest. You’ve been through a lot, but the worst is over. All you have to do now is heal. I’ll check on you a little later .” Nancy walked away, slipping through the curtain . “It hurts so much,” she whispered, touching her side . The bed creaked as Ronin sat on the edge, placing his hand over Lara’s. With his other hand, he delicately ran his fingertips over her cheek, brushing strands of hair behind her ear. “I thought I was going to lose you, Lara Brooks .” Lara met his gaze. She’d never seen such agony in his eyes. She turned her hand over and laced her fingers with his. Her memories of what had happened were unclear, but she’d heard the gunshots, had watched the light fade from his gaze . “I did lose you,” she said, voice hoarse as her throat tightened and tears welled in her eyes . His smile was sorrow-tinged, but genuine. “Now I understand helplessness and fear .” The tears rolled down her cheeks. “I love you .” “Love you, too.” He wiped away the moisture with his thumbs. “Rest. I’ll be here when you wake again, and I’ll tell you everything .” Though she longed to stay awake, to stare at him and touch him and hear his voice, the laudanum was taking effect. The aches that wracked her body dulled, and the edges of her consciousness grew fuzzy. The bedding dipped, and Ronin’s warmth enveloped her as he laid down beside her . Sleep claimed Lara . * * * “W hat’s wrong?” Ronin asked . Lara looked up from the bowl in his hand, dropping the spoon into it. She’d spent two days in constant discomfort and pain. The drowsiness and disorientation caused by the laudanum had lingered a long while after she woke, and she refused more when Nancy brought it. She wanted to remain awake and alert. Wanted the reminder that she was alive and healing . “Why didn’t Warlord kill me ?” The skin between Ronin’s brows creased. “As far as he was concerned, he did .” “He hunted us down like animals. Why not finish it? He killed Tabitha.” Her throat constricted. “Why not me , too ?” “What he did to Tabitha was meant as an example to everyone in Cheyenne. She…suffered, but he ended it. With us, he was only concerned about the suffering. There was no one around to witness it, and no one from Cheyenne would ever find us there. He wanted us broken, wanted you to die slowly and in pain, all alone .” The four, quick gunshots rang out in her memory. If it hadn’t been for Newton and the spare power supplies in the shelter, she’d be dead in the Dust. The twenty feet between her and Ronin’s deactivated casing might as well have been a thousand miles . “What did he do to her?” Lara asked quietly. “What example did he make ?” He was quiet, eyes dropping . “Ronin. I deserve to know .” For a few more seconds, she didn’t think he’d reply. “They beat her to death. Her body was covered in bruises, her facial bones shattered, blood matting her hair. After they dismantled the synth, they laid his face over her groin and put his penis in her mouth .” She grabbed his wrist, and he lifted his gaze to meet hers. The sudden action sent stabbing pain through her side. Despite the bindings around her torso, it still hurt like a son of a bitch. “Why didn’t you tell me, Ronin? Why did you bring me to the Bot District in the first place ?” “I didn’t know, Lara. Didn’t even know, for sure, after I found her. By then, my only objective was to purchase enough supplies to get you far away from there. Her death affected you so much, already, that I didn’t want to heap more pain atop it .” Her head throbbed. He’d been trying to spare her more pain, trying to prevent her from collapsing beneath the weight of her loss. “Warlord could have killed you .” “Takes more than a change of batteries to keep me down .” Despite the nature of their conversation, Lara would have laughed. But laughter was too painful due to her ribs. She shook her head. “I can’t believe you choose now to joke .” “You’re alive and recovering. What better a time for smiles ?” “Even smiling hurts. Bet I look like shit.” She recalled how she’d looked and felt when Warlord raped her. Her skin had been discolored with bruises, eyes and lips swollen. She’d stared at herself in Tabitha’s little mirror, and didn’t recognize the face staring back; by the tenderness of her skin now, she had to look worse . “Not nearly as bad as I did, after I blew up,” Ronin replied. “And you’re always beautiful to me, regardless .” “You always say the nicest things.” Her eyes stung. “You’re gonna make me leak again .” She took hold of the spoon and lifted some of the soup to her mouth; even that simple act was agonizing. The soup was already cool, but still flavorful — an assortment of vegetables in a spiced broth . “Why did you have to wake while I was gone?” Ronin asked after she ate a few more mouthfuls. “Every moment I wasn’t being questioned by the leadership, I spent here with you, waiting for you to open your eyes .” Setting the spoon down, she took his hand in hers and guided it to her cheek. “I remember your voice. Don’t leave yet . I focused on those words, held on because of them. When I woke up, there were people arguing. Nancy and a man…Dan, or Dave, I think .” “ Arguing ?” “Yeah. They were fighting about me taking up too many resources, and whether I’d wake up or not .” Ronin frowned deeply. “It’s a rational argument for them to make. They’ve got limited supplies, just like everyone else. But, when it comes to talk of letting you die, rationality can go fuck itself. I wouldn’t have let them .” Lara smiled, pressing her lips to his palm. “I know. Nancy pretty much told him that , too .” He placed the bowl on the bedside stand and leaned forward, kissing her . The curtain parted, and Nancy stepped in. “How is our patient today ?” Ronin eased back onto his chair . “Alive, sore, and wanting to get the hell out of this bed,” Lara said. Not that she looked forward to the pain of getting up . Nancy took gently hold of Lara’s wrist and stood silently for a few moments. “I think that can be arranged,” she said, and bent down to examine Lara’s face. “The bruises are fading, and you still have some swelling, but overall you’re healing well. You need to take it easy on the ribs, but I think it’d do you some good for Ronin to give you a tour of the base .” “I’d like that .” “I’ll be back in a moment , then .” Nancy walked away, and Lara looked to Ronin. “How big is this place, anyway ?” “I don’t know,” he replied. “I haven’t seen all of it yet. Doubt I ever will. It’s big .” Lara grinned, the skin around her mouth pulling tight against her still-healing cuts. “I bet not knowing bothers you quite a bit .” “I understand their reasoning. But yes, it bothers me. There must be multiple entrances, but if we had to get out, I only know of the way we came in .” The curtain rings scraped over the rod when Nancy returned, flicking them open. She pushed a chair with large wheels into the partition. “This should help you get around .” “What’s that?” Lara asked . “A wheelchair. You’re not going to make it very far on your feet, in your current state .” Lara grunted, and the blast of pain from her ribs had her immediately regretting it. Nancy had forced her out of bed every day since she woke. Every step, every breath, had been agony. Before she even reached the curtain, she’d be ready to return to bed. Now she was going to be wheeled around the base? It was degrading . Ronin drew the blanket down and offered Lara his hand. After a few careful breaths, she took it, and slid her legs over the side of the bed. He placed his other hand on the small of her back, supporting her as she stood . Slowly, she eased into the wheelchair, tucking her splinted arm against her middle . Nancy brushed her fingers over Lara’s braid. “Enjoy yourself. I’ll see you back here later .” Ronin pushed her out of the room — he said it was called the Infirmary — and followed the green line on the floor along a large, rounded, concrete tunnel. The muffled voices of a crowd drifted down the corridor. They passed several people, many of whom cast friendly smiles in Lara’s direction. It was strange; in the human settlement back in Cheyenne, direct eye contact was sometimes considered a challenge. It ended, too often, with a knife buried in someone’s gut . But these people just… smiled . They all looked healthy, if pale, like they’d never missed a meal in their lives. Their clothing was clean and well-mended. Their skin wasn’t weather-damaged, and they didn’t have grime under their fingernails . This is what I was becoming, after Ronin took me home . The voices grew louder as they emerged from the tunnel into a huge room. There were dozens of people all around, engaged in various activities — many of which Lara had no name for — and conversations. Pictures flickered on screens here and there on the walls, and machinery thrummed, buzzed, and clanked . “There’re so many people,” Lara said, eyes shifting from face to face . “Not all human,” Ronin said . “There are bots here , too ?” “I’ve counted thirty-three, but I know there are more .” She looked over the crowd, spotting a few skinless bots. If there were more synths present, she couldn’t tell them apart from the humans. They all spoke with their hands as much as their mouths, laughed together, wore their emotions on their faces . It was nothing like Cheyenne. These people were not divided . They lived in harmony . A line of six men in matching clothes marched out of a tunnel ahead, each carrying a rifle. More weaponry dangled from their belts — pistols and knives . “Who are they?” she asked . “ Soldiers .” “Like the gearheads, only human ?” “Not all human,” he repeated, “and not much like the gearheads. They’re here to protect the people who live here, not to hold them under the thumbs of their leaders .” Lara followed the soldiers’ progress until they disappeared in the crowd. “How many of them are there ?” “I don’t know. They trust me enough to let me walk around the common areas, but I haven’t been able to get an accurate gauge of their real numbers or armament. They don’t flaunt it , here .” She frowned; now that she knew their clothes, she spotted three more of them on the walkways overhead . Ronin turned down another tunnel, and as they moved further in, the air grew moist. The smells of wet earth and vegetation strengthened, and soon they entered another large room . Rows upon rows of plants and trees stood beneath bright, warm lights. The air itself seemed thick with life . Dew drops glistened on the vibrant green leaves. Brightly colored fruit hung from the branches, and vegetables grew closer to the ground. Many of them were unfamiliar to her . Stopping beside a tree, Ronin reached up and plucked a small, orange fruit. He handed it to her. It was smaller than her palm, with a soft, fuzzy skin . “What is it ?” “An apricot .” She stared at it for a moment before raising it to her lips. Her teeth sank easily into the tender flesh, and sweetness exploded over her tongue. It was the most wonderful thing she’d ever tasted . And yet…she could barely swallow it. She swept her gaze over the tree, branches filled with more apricots than she could count, and then over the surrounding trees, all equally full . “ Like it ?” “ It’s good .” “Your tone says otherwise.” Ronin stepped to the front of the wheelchair and crouched to Lara’s eye-level, his brows low. “What’s wrong ?” “It’s a whole ‘nother world down here.” She gestured to the greenery. “I’ve never seen so much food in my entire life, you know ?” He turned his head, following her gesture, and then met her gaze again. “And this… upsets you ?” Lara looked down at the apricot and brushed her thumb over its skin. Before she could respond, several loud moos filled the air . “ Cows ?” “Yes, and more.” He rose and returned to the rear of the chair. He pushed her beyond the plants, toward an opening that led into another room. The smell from within was far more pungent and less pleasant . The cows stood in a penned-off area, heads bowed and tails flicking from side to side as they chewed grass. A man sat on a stool beside one. He tugged on its udders, spraying milk into a bucket . “There was an old man back in Cheyenne who had a cow, when I was a little girl. Its skin was stretched over its bones, and its eyes…its eyes were always so sad .” She scowled at the fat beasts. “Someone tried to steal it one night, and they shot the man when he tried to stop them. Next day, they’d already butchered it and sold the meat .” Ronin was silent, but she could almost sense the questions brimming within him . Chickens wandered closer, clucking while they bobbed their heads, and sheep and goats bleated farther back . Lara clenched her fist in her lap. “I want to go back.” She wanted to scream, wanted to rage, but she couldn’t . Without a word, Ronin obeyed. He rolled her back the way they’d come, smoothly, not jarring her aching body a single time . Nancy was waiting in the Infirmary, along with a tall, darker-skinned man. He turned to face Lara as Ronin wheeled her inside . “Did it feel good to get out of this room, for a change?” Nancy asked. When Lara didn’t respond, Nancy tilted her head to the side, frowning. “This is Colonel Jack Rodriguez. He’s Head of Security, and he’s been talking a lot with Ronin since you two arrived .” “You must be Miss Brooks,” Rodriguez said. “Now that you’re feeling better, I have some questions for you .” Lara held his gaze. Anger burned in her gut; the way this man stood, his confidence and air of authority, reminded her of Warlord . “Lara?” Ronin asked from behind her . “Why didn’t you help us?” she asked, voice level . “You’re alive because we helped you,” Rodriguez replied. His already stoic expression hardened . “Why didn’t you fucking help us?” She ignored the pain of each breath, unable to tamp down her rage. “You left us to them! Left us to starve— to die !” “I don’t know what the fuck you’re talking about, Miss Brooks , but — ” “Cheyenne! All those people. You left us to sift through the fucking Dust, trying to find enough shit to earn a few bites of food from that bastard when this whole time you had food and shelter and medicine here. You have protection here !” She didn’t realize that she’d stood up and stepped toward Rodriguez until Ronin’s hands settled on her shoulders and stopped her forward movement . Rodriguez didn’t back away, didn’t break eye contact. “You’ve been through a lot, and I can’t pretend I know what it’s like .” “And you did nothing! My sister is dead, hundreds of people are dead, and you might as well be responsible for it. You let that monster run free, while you live here in luxury .” “We work for everything we have down here. Every. Fucking. Thing. Every time someone eats a piece of fruit,” his eyes dropped to the crushed apricot in her trembling fist, “we have to worry about whether we’ll be able to produce another to take its place. Every time someone needs medical attention, there’s a chance we’ll never be able to replace the supplies used to care for them. We have a lot of our own people to worry about right here, Miss Brooks. We’re not responsible for the rest of the damned world .” “Fuck you!” she screamed, chest heaving. Agony pierced her entire being. Black spots dotted her vision . “ Fuck …” “That’s enough, dammit!” Nancy rushed forward . Lara’s legs gave out, but Ronin was there, taking her sagging weight into his arms. He carried her to the bed and laid her down carefully . “You need to drink this now,” Nancy said. Something cool pressed to Lara’s lips . It smelled sharply of alcohol and something else; the sleep of the dead . “No. I don’t…” Lara swiped at it, but Nancy was quicker, moving the cup before it was knocked out of her hand . “Tilt her head back, and plug her nose .” Ronin’s hands followed Nancy’s orders, too strong to fight, but somehow gentle. Lara stared up at him, unable to believe the betrayal . “This will dull the pain and calm you, before you do any lasting harm to yourself,” he said, words oddly strained . Tears stung her eyes, and she averted her gaze. The moment she opened her mouth to take a breath, Nancy poured the foul liquid in, and covered Lara’s lips to prevent her from spitting it out . With no other choice, Lara swallowed . “She’s not going to get out of it,” Rodriguez said. “I expect to be notified when she’s well enough to be questioned, Doctor Cooper.” His footfalls were heavy as he walked away . “I’m sorry, Lara, I really am,” Nancy said, pursing her lips as she and Ronin removed their hands. “You need to rest. Once you’re stronger, you can berate Jack all you want, but as your doctor, I can’t allow you to hurt yourself.” She brushed a stray lock of hair back from Lara’s temple, glanced briefly at Ronin, and left . Ronin’s fingers slipped around Lara’s right hand, but she jerked it out of his hold . “ Don’t .” “I vowed to protect you, Lara Brooks,” he said, touching her chin and gently guiding her face toward him. She met his eyes before he leaned forward and brushed his lips over her forehead. “I’m not going to fail to uphold that vow again .”
